description: NiO AFM-II linear-response Hubbard U vs QE 7.5 hp.x (DFPT)
note: hp.x needs the two-step insulator procedure (smeared SCF, then occupations='fixed' with tot_magnetization=0 and nbnd=24, then hp.x with U_in=1e-8); the single-step smeared run aborts on the metallic Fermi-shift term. nq=1,1,1 (Gamma-only perturbation) matches gradwave's single-cell finite-difference response. gradwave linear_response_u gives 6.4493 eV (0.3% off).
